steps.title: Identify the x-intercept from the graph
steps.content: The line represents all possible combinations of M-type and K-type stars that produce a total mass of 129,492 quettagrams. Reading from the graph, the line crosses the x-axis at $(160, 0)$, where there are 160 M-type stars and 0 K-type stars.

steps.title: Set up the mass equation at the x-intercept
steps.content: When $y = 0$ (no K-type stars), all the total mass comes from M-type stars alone. If each M-type star has mass $m$, then: $$160m = 129,492$$

steps.title: Solve for the mass of each M-type star
steps.content: Dividing both sides by 160: $$m = \frac{129,492}{160} = 809.325 \text{ quettagrams}$$ Comparing to the choices, 809.325 is closest to 830. That matches choice A — A is correct.
